subtitle: Where gold pagodas, desert horses and distant seas met beneath Hampi’s temples

panels.shot: sweeping bird’s-eye over Hampi’s temple-lined bazaar streets
panels.action: A vast stone-paved street runs from a towering Vijayanagara temple through colonnaded bazaars, tiny specks of Vijayanagara Merchant figures moving among stalls heavy with textiles, gems and gold under a hot Deccan sky.

dialogue.text: In the age of Vijayanagara, merchants of Hampi carried diamonds, horses and gold pagodas between sacred streets and the wide, restless seas.

i18n.hi: विजयनगर के दौर में हम्पी के सौदागर पवित्र गलियों से उछलते समुद्रों तक हीरे, घोड़े और सोने के पगोडे ले जाते थे

panels.shot: slow push-in along the ruined colonnades, imagining them alive
panels.action: Vijayanagara Merchant stands in rich cotton robes and simple jewelry, gazing down a long colonnaded bazaar street whose stalls are crowded with goods, temple towers rising at one end, hills and boulders at the other.

dialogue.text: Travelers from Persia and Portugal wrote in wonder: that in Hampi, the very streets of the gods were markets, and wealth walked openly beneath carved stone.

i18n.hi: फारस और पुर्तगाल के यात्री हैरान लिख गए: हम्पी में देवताओं की गलियाँ ही बाज़ार थीं, तराशी पत्थर तले दौलत खुलकर चलती थी

panels.shot: wide shot of Abdur Razzaq-like foreign observer watching bazaar bustle
panels.action: Beside Vijayanagara Merchant, a bearded Persian traveler in long robes watches as jewel dealers sit on low platforms, baskets of uncut stones and strings of pearls laid before them, while scribes scratch tallies on palm leaves.

dialogue.text: Abdur Razzaq of Herat saw jewel sellers seated in rows, rubies and pearls poured like grain, and wrote that riches here were spread as if the earth itself had opened.

i18n.hi: हेरात के अब्दुर रज़्ज़ाक ने देखा कतारों में बैठे जौहरी, rubies और मोती अनाज–से बिखरे, और लिखा कि जैसे खुद ज़मीन फटकर दौलत निकाल दे

panels.shot: medium close-up on merchant’s hands sorting gems
panels.action: Vijayanagara Merchant kneels behind a low desk, his weathered hands calmly separating small piles of colored stones, weighing them on a simple balance as a local woman watches, her face intent and confident.

dialogue.text: The gems came from many lands: diamonds from inland mines, sapphires and rubies by longer routes, pearls from southern seas. Hampi’s merchants did not summon them; they wove them into a web of trade.

i18n.hi: रत्न कई देसों से आते थे: अंदरूनी खदानों से हीरे, दूर राहों से नीलम–माणिक, दक्षिणी समुंदरों से मोती। हम्पी के व्यापारी उन्हें बुलाते नहीं थे, सौदों की जाल में पिरोते थे

panels.shot: low-angle view down the bazaar with temple at the end, crowds dense
panels.action: The street is packed with weavers showing bright cloth, spice sellers with sacks of pepper and cardamom, and Vijayanagara Merchant moving among them, his eyes measuring, greeting fellow traders with respectful nods.

dialogue.text: Foreigners saw not just jewels, but cottons, silks, spices, rice and oil. These bazaars fed the city and linked it to Coromandel looms, Malabar forests and distant harbors.

i18n.hi: विदेशी सिर्फ गहने नहीं, सूती कपड़े, रेशम, मसाले, चावल और तेल भी देखते थे। यही बाज़ार शहर को खिलाते और उसे कोरोमंडल के करघों, मलाबार के जंगलों और दूर बंदरगाहों से जोड़ते थे

panels.shot: tight close-up on a shining gold pagoda in palm
panels.action: In a pool of warm light, Vijayanagara Merchant’s palm holds a small, thick gold coin, its surface bearing a simple standing figure, the edges worn smooth from countless exchanges.

dialogue.text: At the heart of this web lay the gold varaha, the pagoda. Heavy, trusted, it passed from temple treasury to merchant chest, from inland caravans to salt-scented ports.

i18n.hi: इस जाल के बीच में था सोने का वराह, पगोडा। भारी, भरोसेमंद, जो मंदिर कोष से व्यापारी के संदूक, काफिलों से नमक–गंधे बंदरों तक घूमता था

panels.shot: tabletop composition of coins, scales, and cloth
panels.action: On a wooden table, Vijayanagara Merchant weighs gold pagodas on a beam-scale, stacks of coins beside bundles of dyed cloth and a ledger on palm leaf, while a younger assistant watches and learns.

dialogue.text: The pagoda was high in value. A few coins settled large accounts: for fine cloth, for loads of pepper, for rights to collect revenue from fields beyond the horizon.

i18n.hi: पगोडा की क़ीमत ऊँची थी। चंद सिक्कों से बड़े हिसाब चुकते होते: महीन कपड़े के, काली मिर्च के ढेरों के, आसमान पार खेतों की लगान के हक़ के

panels.shot: transition shot from bazaar to riverbank with boats
panels.action: Vijayanagara Merchant stands by the Tungabhadra’s rocky bank, watching long, low boats being loaded with bales and chests, temple towers faint behind him and the open river leading away.

dialogue.text: From Hampi, roads and rivers carried goods outward. Inland traders met coastal brokers, and pagodas traveled to the Malabar pepper ports and the busy Coromandel towns.

i18n.hi: हम्पी से सड़कें और नदियाँ माल बाहर ले जाती थीं। भीतर के सौदागर तट के दलालों से मिलते, और पगोडे मलाबार के मिर्च बंदरों और चहल–पहल वाले कोरोमंडल कस्बों तक चलते जाते

panels.shot: wide shot of a bustling Coromandel port town
panels.action: Vijayanagara Merchant is now in a crowded Coromandel harbor, watching masts and lateen sails in the bay, port warehouses stacked with textiles, while local brokers argue over bales of blue-dyed cloth.

dialogue.text: On the eastern coast, Coromandel ports turned cotton into ocean wares. Cloth from these looms clothed sailors in Hormuz, merchants in Malacca, and even some in Lisbon.

i18n.hi: पूरब के तट पर कोरोमंडल के बंदर कपास को समुंदरी माल बनाते थे। इन करघों का कपड़ा होर्मुज़ के मल्लाह, मलक्का के सौदागर, यहाँ तक कि लिस्बन के कुछ लोग पहनते थे

panels.shot: medium shot of merchant meeting a Portuguese factor in Goa-style setting
panels.action: In a Goan courtyard with laterite walls and a small church tower beyond, Vijayanagara Merchant sits opposite a Portuguese factor in plain European dress, both leaning over a tray of coins and a map of western coasts.

dialogue.text: After the Portuguese seized Goa, their factors became new partners and rivals. Between them and Vijayanagara’s traders, gold and horses moved with care and calculation.

i18n.hi: पुर्तगालियों ने गोवा छीनने के बाद उनके कारकुन नए साथी–मुक़ाबिल बने। उन्हीं और विजयनगर के व्यापारियों के बीच सोना और घोड़े नाप–तौल कर चलते रहे

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle of a ship unloading horses at a western port
panels.action: At a western coastal anchorage, a broad-hulled ship lies close to shore; rampways lead down as sleek, nervous Arabian horses are guided to land, Vijayanagara Merchant watching from a safe distance.

dialogue.text: From Arabia and Persia came what the empire could not breed enough: horses for its cavalry. Sanjay Subrahmanyam showed that this was no detail, but a deep dependence on far coasts.

i18n.hi: अरब और फारस से आता था जो सल्तनत खुद नहीं पाल पाती थी: घुड़सवार फौज के घोड़े। संजय सुब्रह्मण्यम ने दिखाया कि यह बस जुमला नहीं, दूर तटों पर गहरी निर्भरता थी

panels.shot: close shot of horse trader and merchant bargaining, with wary horses behind
panels.action: Under a palm-frond shelter, Vijayanagara Merchant and an Arab horse trader sit facing each other, hands poised, while tied horses toss their heads in the background, attendants struggling to calm them.

dialogue.speaker: vijayanagara_merchants
dialogue.text: Good mounts must reach our fields, not die on your ships.

i18n.hi: अच्छे घोड़े हमारे खेतों तक पहुँचें, तुम्हारी नावों में मरें नहीं

panels.shot: medium shot of merchant offering a small casket and coins to a coastal broker
panels.action: Vijayanagara Merchant opens a small wooden casket filled with gold pagodas and a few larger jewels before a coastal broker seated cross-legged, both men intent, a rolled contract beside them.

dialogue.text: Horses cost dearly. Gold and sometimes gems paid for them. The flows ran both ways: wealth from inland fields and bazaars went out; living power on hoof came in.

i18n.hi: घोड़े बेहद महँगे थे। सोना और कभी–कभी रत्न उनकी क़ीमत भरते। रेला दो तरफ़ बहता था: खेतों–बाज़ारों की दौलत बाहर जाती, खुरों पर सवार ज़िंदा ताकत अंदर आती

panels.shot: wide interior shot of a royal stable filled with horses
panels.action: Inside a royal stable with vaulted stone roofs, rows of strong horses stand tethered, grooms attending them, while Vijayanagara Merchant watches a noble inspecting the animals with practiced eye.

dialogue.text: Foreign chroniclers gave huge numbers for yearly horse imports, perhaps too large. Yet the gathered stables show the truth behind the figures: cavalry strength depended on these distant trades.

i18n.hi: विदेशी लेखक सालाना घोड़ा–आयात के लिए बहुत बड़े आंकड़े लिख गए, शायद ज़्यादा। पर जमा अस्तबल बताते हैं कि गिनतियों के पीछे सच क्या था: घुड़सवार फौज की ताकत इन्हीं दूर सौदों पर टिकी थी

panels.shot: split composition: bazaar on one side, distant battlefield haze on the other
panels.action: A visual diptych: Vijayanagara Merchant weighs pagodas in a bright bazaar on the left, while on the right, hazy mounted warriors line up on a distant field, dust hanging, spears upright.

dialogue.text: In these coins, markets and ships lay the sinews of war. Pagodas in trader hands became pay for soldiers, fodder for horses, timber for fort walls.

i18n.hi: इन्हीं सिक्कों, बाज़ारों और जहाज़ों में जंग की नसें थीं। सौदागर के हाथों के पगोडे ही सिपाहियों की तनख्वाह, घोड़ों का चारा, किले की लकड़ियाँ बनते थे

panels.shot: intimate interior of merchant’s house with family, chests and account leaves
panels.action: In a modest yet well-kept home, Vijayanagara Merchant sits with family members around chests, opening one to reveal coins and cloth samples, while a younger relative practices writing accounts on palm leaves.

dialogue.text: Merchants were not just numbers in royal ledgers. Their households lived by risk and trust: ships might fail, rulers might fall, but accounts and kin had to endure.

i18n.hi: व्यापारी सिर्फ शाही खातों के अंक नहीं थे। उनके घराने जोखिम और भरोसे पर जीते थे: जहाज़ डूब सकते थे, बादशाह बदल सकते थे, पर हिसाब–किताब और रिश्ते टिकने ही पड़ते थे

panels.shot: wide shot of bazaars near temple, with pilgrims and traders mingling
panels.action: Pilgrims walk toward the towering temple, while Vijayanagara Merchant and other traders arrange their wares along the sacred road, cows ambling between stalls, the air thick with incense and the murmur of bargaining.

dialogue.text: In Hampi, temples were not apart from trade. Bazaars lined their axes. Offerings and profits moved side by side, tying devotion to the everyday work of exchange.

i18n.hi: हम्पी में मंदिर सौदे से अलग नहीं थे। उनकी धुर्रियों पर बाज़ार सजे थे। चढ़ावे और मुनाफे साथ–साथ चलते, भक्ति को रोज़मर्रा के लेन–देन से बाँधते थे

panels.shot: melancholic wide shot of today’s ruins, imagined merchant ghosted within
panels.action: The same colonnaded street now lies quiet in ruin, stone pillars empty under the sun; a faint, almost ghost-like image of Vijayanagara Merchant stands at the center, looking down the bare avenue.

dialogue.text: The empire fell in the sixteenth century, its bazaars hushed. Yet the memory of their reach still lives in coins in museums, in Portuguese and Persian pages, in stones that once saw crowds.

i18n.hi: सल्तनत सोलहवीं सदी में ढही, उसके बाज़ार थम गए। पर उनकी उड़ान की याद आज भी अँगरों में रखे सिक्कों में, पुर्तगाली–फारसी किताबों में, उन पत्थरों में है जिन्होंने कभी भीड़ देखी थी

panels.shot: closing close-up on merchant’s thoughtful face fading into a map of sea routes
panels.action: Vijayanagara Merchant’s calm, weathered face fills the frame, then subtly blends into a simple map of the Indian Ocean, with coasts of India, Arabia and the Gulf marked in faint lines.

dialogue.text: Their legacy is a lesson in connected strength: an inland city bound by trust to coasts and caravans, where prosperity rose from wise dependence and the courage to trade across the unknown.

i18n.hi: उनकी विरासत जुड़ी ताकत का सबक है: अंदरूनी शहर जो भरोसे से तटों और काफिलों से बंधा था, जहाँ समृद्धि दूर–दराज़ पर निर्भर रहने की समझ और अनदेखे में सौदा करने के हौसले से उगी

title_i18n.en: Diamonds Horses and Pagodas in the Bazaar
title_i18n.hi: हीरे, घोड़े और पगोडे बाज़ार में

subtitle_i18n.en: Where gold pagodas, desert horses and distant seas met beneath Hampi’s temples
subtitle_i18n.hi: जहाँ सुनहरे पगोडे, रेगिस्तानी घोड़े और दूर समुंदर हम्पी के मंदिरों तले मिलते थे
